summary.zh-CN: 聚焦 S01E01 场景，提取中级学习者可迁移的高价值词汇与短语。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hank Mm. Well, it's not the most we ever took. It's easy money, till we catch you.
sourceContext.targetLine: Hank Walt, just say the word and I'll take you on a ride-along.
sourceContext.nextLine: You watch us knock down a meth lab, huh? Get a little excitement in your life.

definitions: 跟警察出勤旁观；随队见习
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克半开玩笑地说一句，你开口我就带你一起出勤见识见识。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是 Walter 生日聚会后看 DEA 缉毒新闻时，Hank 对 Walt 说的话。ride-along 在美国警务语境里不是单纯“兜风”，而是让非警员跟车旁观执法现场，既显得熟门熟路，也带一点 Hank 式炫耀阳刚感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常见结构是 take someone on a ride-along。它很有美国警务和媒体语感，别直译成 ride together。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hank Uh, it's about 700 grand. It was a pretty good haul.
sourceContext.targetLine: Hank (on TV) As I say, it's a good day for the citizens of Albuquerque—where we can put this big a dent in the local drug trade.
sourceContext.nextLine: Walter Well, that's unusual, isn't it? That kind of cash?

definitions: 对…造成明显打击；削弱一大块
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他说这次查获，算是对本地毒品交易狠狠打掉了一块。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Hank 在电视采访里把执法成果包装成政绩话术。put a dent in 带的是“还没根除，但终于打出点效果”的语气，很像新闻发言或工作汇报里常见的美国表达。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 不只用于犯罪话题，也常说 put a dent in debt, put a dent in the workload。比 solve 更现实，强调只是削弱，不是彻底解决。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter Bogdan, no. We talked about this.
sourceContext.targetLine: Bogdan (Man) I'm short-handed, Walter. What am I to do?
sourceContext.nextLine: Bogdan Walter?

definitions: 人手不够的
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Bogdan 拿“现在缺人”当理由，逼 Walt 继续顶班干活。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这一幕发生在洗车店，Bogdan 用老板口气把自己说成没办法的人。short-handed 是美国职场里非常高频的缺人说法，背后常带着“所以你得补位”的压迫感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常说 We're short-handed today. 用在餐厅、零售、医院都很自然。它比 understaffed 更口语，也更像现场抱怨。

sourceContext.previousLine: Bogdan Come on, I'm shorthanded. I need you outside to do some wipe downs. Come on!
sourceContext.targetLine: Bogdan I said I need you outside to do some wipe downs. Are you hear to work, or to be staring at the skies?
sourceContext.nextLine: Walter Fuck you, Bogdan.

definitions: 把表面擦一遍；做收尾清洁
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Bogdan 催他出去做擦车收尾，语气里全是使唤和羞辱。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里不是一般的 clean，而是洗车店里那种机械、低技术含量的擦拭杂活。对白之所以刺耳，是因为 Walt 刚确诊癌症，还得被前学生围观、被老板赶去做最没体面的工序。
zh-CN.usageNotes: wipe down 常接 table, counter, machine 这类表面。口语里 do a quick wipe-down 很常见，表示随手清一遍，不是彻底打扫。

sourceContext.previousLine: Physician You stood what I've just said to you?
sourceContext.targetLine: Walter Yes. Lung cancer, inoperable.
sourceContext.nextLine: Physician I'm sorry. I just need to make sure you fully understand.

definitions: 无法手术的；已不能通过手术处理的
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 机械地复述诊断结果：肺癌，已经没法开刀了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 医生在确认他有没有真正听懂病情，Walt 却像背术语一样把最残酷的信息说出来。inoperable 是医疗场景里的重词，短、冷、专业，正因为医生不拐弯才更有冲击力。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 几乎专用于医疗语境，和 broken、not working 那种日常 meaning 不同。听到 inoperable tumor / cancer，通常就意味着治疗选择明显变窄。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter So, what tells you it's a meth lab?
sourceContext.targetLine: Hank Just our snitch. Says, uh, some dude who goes by "Cap'n Cook" lives up to his name in there.
sourceContext.nextLine: Says he always adds a dash of chili powder.

definitions: 线人；给警方通风报信的人
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克很随意地说，就是我们的线人给的消息。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在缉毒车里，Hank 用 snitch 这种街头味很重的词，而不是 informant，说明他讲话一直带执法圈里的粗口化风格。对学习者来说，懂这个词能直接补上美剧里警匪语域的差别。
zh-CN.usageNotes: snitch 带明显贬义，像在骂人出卖同伴。正式场合更常说 informant。别把它用在普通“告老师”以外太轻松的语境里。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter So, what tells you it's a meth lab?
sourceContext.targetLine: Hank Just our snitch. Says, uh, some dude who goes by "Cap'n Cook" lives up to his name in there.
sourceContext.nextLine: Says he always adds a dash of chili powder.

definitions: 以…为名；别人都这么叫
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他说那家伙外号叫“Cap'n Cook”。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里的 go by 不是“经过”或“时间流逝”，而是身份称呼义。警察在盯梢时用这个说法，是在讲嫌疑人平时在圈子里用什么外号活动。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常说 I go by Mike 或 He goes by Cap'n Cook。比 my name is 更像介绍昵称、英文名、外号或圈内叫法。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter Phosphine gas. I think.
sourceContext.targetLine: Hank Yeah, exactly. One whiff will kill you. That's why the respirators.
sourceContext.nextLine: Man (on radio) House is clear. One suspect in custody.

definitions: 一小口气味；一点点闻到的量
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克说这种气体只要闻上一口，人就可能没命，所以才必须戴呼吸防护。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: DEA 在车里解释毒品实验室风险时，Hank 用 one whiff 把危险说得又口语又具体。它让观众立刻感到这不是抽象化学知识，而是现场真会致命的东西。
zh-CN.usageNotes: whiff 常跟 of 连用，既能指真闻到一点味，也能引申成一点迹象。One whiff of trouble 这种说法也很常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: Pinkman Why are you here?
sourceContext.targetLine: Walter I was curious. Honestly, I never expected you to amount to much... but methamphetamine? I— I didn't picture that.
sourceContext.nextLine: Pinkman I don't know what you're talking about.

definitions: 有多大出息；成得了什么气候
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 很伤人地说：老实讲，我本来没指望你能成什么事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Walter 找 Jesse 谈合作时，先用老师对差生的老眼光压他一下。amount to much 在这里不是客观评价能力，而是带社会阶层和优越感的贬低，听上去很刺人。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常见于否定句：never amount to much, won't amount to anything。语气很重，容易冒犯，通常是在批评别人没前途。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter No?
sourceContext.targetLine: Pinkman Not a clue.
sourceContext.nextLine: Walter Cap'n Cook? That's not you?

definitions: 完全不知道；一点头绪也没有
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 直接装傻：我完全不知道你在说什么。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Walter 已经把 Jesse 堵到家门口了，Jesse 还是用最短的口语块硬顶回去。not a clue 比 I don't know 更像街头式否认，短、快、带防备心。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 很常单独成句，也可说 I haven't got a clue。语气比 I don't know 更绝对，也常带一点不耐烦或撇清关系。

sourceContext.previousLine: Pinkman I don't know what you think you're doing here, Mr. White. I mean, if you're planning on giving me some... bull-winder about getting right with Jesus or turning myself in.
sourceContext.targetLine: Pinkman High school was a long time ago. Okay, you ain't Welcome Back, Kotter, so step off. No speeches.
sourceContext.nextLine: Walter Short speech.

definitions: 别来管我；离远点
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 说得很直白：别摆老师架子，离我远点，别说教。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这一幕是 Jesse 拒绝被 Walt 重新放回“师生关系”里。step off 在这里不是物理上走开，而是社交边界上的“你别越线”，再加上 Welcome Back, Kotter 这种老校园剧梗，整句都是美国式反说教语气。
zh-CN.usageNotes: step off 很口语，也有点挑衅，常见于冲突或被冒犯时。比 back off 更街头、更不客气。

sourceContext.previousLine: The DEA took all your money, your lab. You've got nothing.
sourceContext.targetLine: Walter Square one. But you know the business and I know the chemistry.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm thinking maybe you and I could partner up.

definitions: 回到起点；从头再来
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 的意思很清楚：你现在被打回原点了，但你还有门路，我有技术。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Walter 在这里像做商业提案一样拆 Jesse 的处境，先把他压到零，再抛出合作方案。square one 是英语里非常高频的“清零重来”说法，剧情里这句也像 Walter 犯罪人生真正从零开局的暗示。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 最常见是 back to square one。适合工作、关系、项目、备考等一切“又得重来”的场景。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter Square one. But you know the business and I know the chemistry.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm thinking maybe you and I could partner up.
sourceContext.nextLine: Pinkman [Scoffs] You uh— You wanna cook crystal meth?

definitions: 搭伙合作；结成搭档
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 试探着抛出核心提议：要不我们俩搭伙。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是整部剧关系结构的起点。partner up 听起来比 do business 更随意，但正因为轻描淡写，反而显出 Walt 在把犯罪包装成一次理性合作。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 可用于工作、项目、课堂分组，也可带一点临时结盟感。和 partner with 相比，partner up 更口语、更像现场决定。

sourceContext.previousLine: Krazy-8 Yeah, that's some stone-fine tick-tick you've been cooking there, ese. How about you come work for me?
sourceContext.targetLine: Walter I'd be willing to sell it to you if— if the price is right.
sourceContext.nextLine: Krazy-8 Out here all by yourself, huh?

definitions: 只要价钱合适就行
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 强装镇定地回：卖给你可以，只要你出的价够高。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是 Walt 第一次真的用交易口吻跟毒贩谈条件。if the price is right 很像美国影视里谈买卖的标准句，表面客气，实际是在放话说“我有得谈，但不会白给”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常用于买卖、接活、授权、出镜、转让等场景。语气比 for enough money 更自然，也常带一点精明和试探。

deckMetadata.title: Breaking Bad · Cat's in the Bag...

summary.zh-CN: 聚焦 S01E02 场景，提取中级学习者可迁移的高价值词汇与短语。

sourceContext.previousLine: Are you gonna help me clean this up?! Huh?!
sourceContext.targetLine: We got loose ends here!
sourceContext.nextLine: Calm down!

definitions: 没处理干净的麻烦尾巴
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 急得直喊：这边还有一堆没收尾的麻烦。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这一集开头 Jesse 在电话里快崩了，因为房车里的人还活着、尸体还没处理。loose ends 很适合这种犯罪或危机情境里“还有漏洞没补完”的感觉，不只是普通待办事项。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 最常见搭配是 tie up loose ends。可用于工作收尾、搬家、分手、法律事务，不一定都像剧里这么危险。

sourceContext.previousLine: A brother-in-law.
sourceContext.targetLine: Oh, well, there's a load off my mind.
sourceContext.nextLine: Where did you hear that?

definitions: 这下我可放心多了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 讽刺地回一句：哦，那我可真是放心死了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Skyler 刚拿 DEA 的关系去警告 Jesse，结果 Walt 还轻描淡写地改口成“只是连襟”。Jesse 这里明显是在说反话，表面像松了口气，实际是在骂 Walt 把他拖进更大的麻烦。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常说 That's a load off my mind. 既能真表示松一口气，也能像这里一样用成反讽。

sourceContext.previousLine: Your freaking wife told me
sourceContext.targetLine: when she was here all up in my shit.
sourceContext.nextLine: Yeah, that's right.

definitions: 跑来对我指手画脚；闯进来管我闲事
zh-CN.corePhrase: be all up in someone's shit
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 抱怨 Skyler 跑到他地盘上来狠狠干涉他的事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是非常 Jesse 的说法，粗、街头、边界感极强。重点不只是“来过”，而是对方越界、逼人、把你的私事翻得一塌糊涂。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这类说法很不正式，也容易冒犯，只适合非常口语、很冲的抱怨语境。更中性的替代是 all up in my business。

term.text: wear the pants in the family
term.lemma: wear the pants in the family

sourceContext.previousLine: She almost caught me moving Emilio!
sourceContext.targetLine: Good job on wearing the pants in the family.
sourceContext.nextLine: And why'd you go and tell her I was selling you weed?

definitions: 在家里说了算；像一家之主那样能做主
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 讽刺 Walt：你可真像家里那个能做主的人啊。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Jesse 在嘲 Walt 根本管不住家里的局面，反而让老婆直接找上门。这个说法带明显老派性别色彩，所以在美剧里常常用来挖苦“你根本没主导权”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: wear the pants 现在常带戏谑或过时性别意味，不适合正式场合。听懂它很重要，但自己用时要知道它可能显得刻薄。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah. Yeah, it is. I smoked a bowl.
sourceContext.targetLine: So what? My house, my rules.
sourceContext.nextLine: Hey, no, no. Hey, don't give me that, okay?

definitions: 在我地盘就按我的规矩来
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 不服管，直接顶回去：那又怎样，我家就按我的规矩来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Walt 明明是在利用 Jesse 的房子处理尸体，却还想摆老师和搭档的架子。Jesse 用这句硬把主场优势夺回来，既是顶嘴，也是划界线。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这是很常见的口语边界表达，家长、室友、朋友都能说。语气通常偏强势，不太有商量余地。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hey, no, no. Hey, don't give me that, okay?
sourceContext.targetLine: I held up my end.
sourceContext.nextLine: I already took care of Emilio.

definitions: 我这边该做的已经做了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 的意思是：我这边答应干的已经干完了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 两个人在互相甩锅时，Jesse 把这件事说成像交易分工。hold up my end 很适合描述合作里“我履行了我那部分”，所以听懂这句能抓住他们关系已经变成利益合伙而不是师生。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常见说法是 hold up my end of the deal / bargain。适用于合作、分工、关系承诺。

sourceContext.previousLine: I held up my end.
sourceContext.targetLine: I already took care of Emilio.
sourceContext.nextLine: You're still diddling around trying to get your nut up.

definitions: 把麻烦处理掉
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 说他已经把 Emilio 处理完了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: take care of 本来可以是“照顾”，但在美剧冲突场景里经常危险得多，可能指摆平问题、解决人、处理后患。这里就是典型的黑暗义项。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这是高频多义词。日常里常指照顾、负责；在犯罪或威胁语境里，语气会变得很重，要靠上下文判断。

sourceContext.previousLine: You see, hydrofluoric acid won't eat through plastic.
sourceContext.targetLine: It will, however, dissolve metal, rock, glass, ceramic.
sourceContext.nextLine: So there's that.

definitions: 溶掉；腐蚀分解
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 冷冷补刀：但它会把金属、石头、玻璃和陶瓷全都溶掉。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是整集最经典的化学恐怖时刻之一。Walt 用老师式的冷静口吻解释 Jesse 为什么把尸体化在浴缸里是灾难，越专业越显得可怕。
zh-CN.usageNotes: dissolve 既能说糖盐“溶解”，也能像这里说材质被化掉。注意它比 melt 更偏化学过程，不只是“融化”。

deckMetadata.title: Breaking Bad · ...And the Bag's in the River

summary.zh-CN: 聚焦 S01E03 场景，提取中级学习者可迁移的高价值词汇与短语。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter So you're saying that Emilio had phosphine gas inhalation?
sourceContext.targetLine: Let's break it down.
sourceContext.nextLine: In order for us to make phosphine, we needed red phosphorus and we needed water.

definitions: 我们一步一步拆开来说
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 说：我们把这件事一条条拆开来讲。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 他在地下室一边审问 Krazy-8，一边像上课一样分析中毒原理。break it down 在这里不是“坏掉”，而是用老师口气把复杂问题拆成可讲清的步骤，这正是 Walt 最可怕也最迷人的地方。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 非常常见于解释、复盘、谈判和吵架。比 explain 更像“拆成简单部分来讲”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Settle down, please. Thank you.
sourceContext.targetLine: I understand that Mr. White is feeling a little under the weather this morning.
sourceContext.nextLine: So you folks are going to have to be making do with me today.

definitions: 身体有点不舒服
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 代课老师说：White 老师今天身体有点不太舒服。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 学校里没人知道 Walt 其实在地下室关着人、精神已经快崩了，所以只用一个很体面的 under the weather 轻轻带过。这种委婉说法正好体现出日常社交里对病情的轻描淡写。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这是非常高频的轻度生病表达，通常比 sick 更柔和，也更适合熟人闲聊。

sourceContext.previousLine: I understand that Mr. White is feeling a little under the weather this morning.
sourceContext.targetLine: So you folks are going to have to be making do with me today.
sourceContext.nextLine: Today you're going to be resuming work on your periodic charts.

definitions: 先凑合用；只能将就着接受
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 代课老师说：今天你们只能先将就着由我来上课了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句很生活化，却很能迁移。学校场景里它表示“没得选，只能先用眼前这个替代方案”，和 Walt 表面正常、内里失控的双线气氛形成反差。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常见于 make do with what you have。适用于预算不够、设备不好、临时替代方案等场景。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter I don't see what real choice you have.
sourceContext.targetLine: If it's between that and cold-blooded murder...
sourceContext.nextLine: Besides, if you kill Krazy-8, you're gonna have to kill me too.

definitions: 蓄意冷血的谋杀
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 回嘴说：这已经是在冷血杀人和别的选择之间二选一了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这一幕是整集道德重心。Jesse 先把事情叫回 murder，而不是“处理问题”，强行把 Walt 从自我合理化里拉回来。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 比 murder 更强调事先清醒、无情、没有情绪失控的借口。新闻、法庭戏、道德谴责里都很常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter Jesse, open the door.
sourceContext.targetLine: Hey, yo, I'm trying to pinch one off in here.
sourceContext.nextLine: Just give me some privacy, would you?

definitions: 拉个屎；上大号
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 在厕所里不耐烦地吼：我正蹲坑呢，给我点隐私行不行？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是非常粗口化、男性化的厕所俚语。它一出来，人物关系立刻不是体面谈判，而是两个被逼到极限的人还在这种荒诞场面里互怼。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 口气很糙，只适合非常随便甚至有点恶心搞笑的语境。正常说法是 take a dump / use the bathroom。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter Jesse, where the hell do you think you're going?
sourceContext.targetLine: Back off, man. Jesus.
sourceContext.nextLine: Walter We've got work to do.

definitions: 别逼我了；离我远点
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 烦到极点，直接吼：别再逼我了，行不行？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句不是礼貌版的 leave me alone，而是被逼急后的防御反应。Jesse 对 Walt 的怨气、恶心和恐惧全压在这两个词里。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 冲突场景高频表达。比 step back 更硬，也带明显情绪。常用于界限被侵犯、被纠缠、被逼问时。

sourceContext.previousLine: Jesse We flipped a coin, okay?
sourceContext.targetLine: Coin flip is sacred.
sourceContext.nextLine: Your job is waiting for you in that basement, as per the coin.

definitions: 掷硬币的结果就得认
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 一本正经地说：既然扔了硬币，结果就是神圣不可违背的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句黑色幽默很重。明明是在决定谁去处理尸体，Walt 却把掷硬币说得像某种庄严契约，既可笑又可怕，也暴露出他越来越依赖伪理性给自己洗白。
zh-CN.usageNotes: sacred 本义是“神圣不可碰”，放到这种日常小事上会带一种夸张、半开玩笑但又不让步的效果。

sourceContext.previousLine: Coin flip is sacred.
sourceContext.targetLine: Your job is waiting for you in that basement, as per the coin.
sourceContext.nextLine: Fuck you. Fuck you and your eyebrows.

definitions: 按…所定；按照…
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 还一本正经地补一句：按硬币决定的规矩，这活现在归你。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: as per 常带一点正式、书面或装腔的味道。Walt 在这种荒谬场景里用它，更像是在给自己套上一层程序正义的外衣。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 商务邮件里很常见，如 as per our conversation。口语里说它会显得比 according to 更书面、更刻意。

summary.zh-CN: 聚焦 S01E04 场景，提取中级学习者可迁移的高价值词汇与短语。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hank I'm thinking Operation Breath Mint.
sourceContext.targetLine: Every time you and me are on a stakeout together, all right?
sourceContext.nextLine: Your breath can knock a buzzard off a shit wagon.

definitions: 蹲点监视
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克吐槽搭档：咱俩每次一起蹲点，你这口气都能熏死人。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: DEA 同事闲聊时提到的 stakeout，是警匪剧里非常核心的职业场景词。它不是普通 watch，而是带目标、带埋伏、常常要长时间盯人的警务动作。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常见于 police stakeout, be on a stakeout。也可引申为记者或私家侦探蹲点。

sourceContext.previousLine: Jesse Yeah, and I don't need no diabetic kid's life story, alright? So what happened?
sourceContext.targetLine: Skinny Pete I heard his cousin bailed him out, but nobody's seen him since. I think he skipped town or maybe he's out in the boonies or something.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse Hey, whatever, man.

definitions: 跑到鸟不拉屎的偏远地方去了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Skinny Pete 猜那人要么跑路了，要么躲到什么特别偏的鬼地方去了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: boonies 是很美国、很口语的“偏得要命的乡下/荒地”。它不是中性地理词，而是带一种“那地方又远又破、谁也找不到”的语感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 非常口语。比 remote area 更生活化，也常带轻微嫌弃或调侃。

sourceContext.previousLine: Elliott Look, Walter, I don't understand. Gretchen and I have so much respect for you.
sourceContext.targetLine: A man of your talents, with your skill set...
sourceContext.nextLine: I mean, come on, if there's anything that we can do to help...

definitions: 一整套专业能力组合
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Elliott 说：像你这种本事、这种能力组合的人，怎么会走到这一步？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句表面在夸 Walt，实际也在提醒他“你本来不该混成这样”。skill set 是很职场、很美式的能力包说法，听懂它能更容易进入美国人谈职业价值的方式。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常用于招聘、升职、合作判断。比 skills 更像“成体系的一套能力”，语气更职业化。

sourceContext.previousLine: Jesse Because I could seriously go for a bowl right now, you know what I'm saying, take the edge off.
sourceContext.targetLine: Take the edge off.
sourceContext.nextLine: Badger Hell, yeah. Sunday night bowl, yo.

definitions: 缓一缓；把紧张感压下去一点
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 说想抽一口，把人那股绷着的劲先压下去。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里不是追求快乐，而是想把压力、焦躁、惊魂未定先削弱一点。这个表达在美剧里很高频，常和酒、药、烟、音乐、休息连用。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 可用于 take the edge off the pain / anxiety / hunger。它强调“减轻一点”，不是彻底解决。

sourceContext.previousLine: Jesse But, you know, I don't know, I've been thinking lately...
sourceContext.targetLine: I may just lay off for a while, because lately it's been kind of making me paranoid, you know?
sourceContext.nextLine: Badger What? I thought you said it wasn't habit-forming.

definitions: 先停一停；暂时别碰
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 说自己可能得先停一阵，不然最近越抽越疑神疑鬼。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这不是正式戒掉，而是口语里那种“我先别碰了”。在朋友聊天里很自然，尤其常用于酒、烟、药、社交媒体、垃圾食品这些会让人上头的东西。
zh-CN.usageNotes: lay off something 比 quit 更轻，通常表示暂时收手。也可用于 lay off the sarcasm，意思是“别再来了”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Skinny Pete Yo, man, maybe I should get moving myself, you know? Getting late. I got... I got plenty of pot.
sourceContext.targetLine: Jesse Yeah, I think I'll bounce, man.
sourceContext.nextLine: Badger Yeah, sounds about right.

definitions: 我先撤了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 说：行，我也先撤了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: bounce 在这里不是“弹跳”，而是年轻人口语里非常自然的 leave。朋友局、派对、尴尬场面、想脱身时都常听到。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 最常见是 I gotta bounce / I'm gonna bounce。语气随意、轻快，比 leave 更有朋友间的松散感。

sourceContext.previousLine: Skyler And we see him on Friday.
sourceContext.targetLine: I mean, Marie really came through for us.
sourceContext.nextLine: She had her boss call, and they managed to work us in.

definitions: 关键时刻真帮上忙了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Skyler 说 Marie 这次真是帮了大忙。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在家人面对癌症初诊、全都慌乱的时候，这句是典型的美式感谢亲属“关键时刻顶上来”。它不只是 helped，而是有种“没掉链子、真靠得住”的情感分量。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 很适合表达对朋友、家人、同事在关键节点的感激。比 help me 更有人情味。

sourceContext.previousLine: Skyler Okay, this is good.
sourceContext.targetLine: From here on out, I mean, things are gonna... this is all gonna get better now.
sourceContext.nextLine: What's that we're putting on the credit card?

definitions: 从现在开始往后
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Skyler 努力安慰自己：从现在开始，事情应该会慢慢变好。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 她在癌症打击里拼命抓住一点“进入治疗流程就会好转”的控制感。from here on out 在家庭、职场、关系里都很常见，常用来给自己或别人立一个新阶段的起点。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 和 from now on 接近，但更口语、更像现场说出口的决定或安慰。

summary.zh-CN: 聚焦 S01E05 场景，提取中级学习者可迁移的高价值词汇与短语。

term.text: get everything out on the table
term.lemma: get everything out on the table

sourceContext.previousLine: Marie Maybe we should just all sit down together and just get everything out on the table.
sourceContext.targetLine: Get everything out in the open.
sourceContext.nextLine: Walter Jr. Totally, man.

definitions: 把话全摊开说清楚
zh-CN.corePhrase: get everything out on the table
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Marie 提议大家坐下来，把所有话都摊开来说。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 全家在讨论 Walt 要不要接受治疗时，场面已经不是医学问题，而是尊严、财务、控制权和秘密一起爆开。get everything out on the table 是很典型的家庭会议/关系谈判表达，意思不是闲聊，而是别再藏着掖着。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 也常说 put everything on the table。适合冲突、谈判、复盘、感情沟通等需要彻底摊牌的场景。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hank We all, in our own way, want to voice our concerns.
sourceContext.targetLine: Voice our concerns.
sourceContext.nextLine: We'll take turns using the talking pillow.

definitions: 把顾虑正式说出来
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克摆出一副很文明的样子，说大家只是想把各自的担心说出来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是典型美国家庭式干预话术，表面温和，其实是在给一个人施加集体压力。voice concerns 比 complain 好听得多，也更像公共讨论或正式表达。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常用于公司会议、家长会、医疗讨论、社区争议。它自带“我是在合理表达担忧，不是在闹情绪”的姿态。

sourceContext.previousLine: Marie Yeah. I mean, why would anybody in their right mind choose not to do the treatment?
sourceContext.targetLine: In their right mind.
sourceContext.nextLine: Especially when it's completely paid for.

definitions: 神志正常的时候；脑子清楚的时候
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Marie 的意思是：神志正常的人，怎么会拒绝治疗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句很重，因为它其实是否定了 Walt 选择的理性。美国家庭冲突里常见这种包装成常识判断的施压：你要是不这么选，就说明你不正常。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常见于 Who in their right mind would...? 语气带强烈不可思议，有时也带冒犯。

sourceContext.previousLine: In their right mind. Especially when it's completely paid for.
sourceContext.targetLine: Especially when it's completely paid for.
sourceContext.nextLine: Why is he doing this?

definitions: 费用已经全包了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Marie 继续补刀：更何况钱都有人出了，你为什么还不治？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 对 Walt 来说，拒绝治疗不只是医疗判断，也和自尊紧紧绑在一起。paid for 在这里看似简单，其实是把“钱的羞耻感”直接推到台面上了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: be paid for 常用于账单、机票、学费、治疗费用。它强调“成本问题已经被解决”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Gretchen But it is good.
sourceContext.targetLine: Walter It isn't good enough.
sourceContext.nextLine: Matter of fact, it's substandard.

definitions: 够不够格；达不达到标准
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 很尖刻地说：这东西不是还行，而是根本不够好。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 他在回忆旧日实验时，用近乎苛刻的标准否掉别人认可的成果。good enough 在这里暴露了 Walt 的完美主义，也解释了他为什么既骄傲又痛苦。
zh-CN.usageNotes: good enough 很常见，但语气关键。说 something isn't good enough 往往不是客观评价，而是强烈挑标准、带压力。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter It isn't good enough. Matter of fact, it's substandard.
sourceContext.targetLine: I understand it. I demand a certain standard.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse You and I both know I would be out of business in a month.

definitions: 我对质量有硬标准
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 的意思很简单：我做事就有我的标准，低于这个水平我不认。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是 Walt 人格核心之一。哪怕在接受施舍、谈治疗、谈旧公司恩怨时，他都还要把自己放回“我要求标准、我配得上更高层次”的位置。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常用于工作质量、服务、产品、职业要求。比 want higher quality 更带权威感和个人原则感。

sourceContext.previousLine: Jesse Why don't we just, like, go our separate ways, all right? And, I don't know, maybe, you know, maybe you can just take the money and run.
sourceContext.targetLine: Take the money and run.
sourceContext.nextLine: Walter Because I'm not done yet.

definitions: 拿了钱就走人；见好就收
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Jesse 劝 Walt：拿着钱收手吧，别再往下折腾了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是非常美式的现实主义建议，意思不是胆小，而是“已经赚到了，就赶紧撤”。Walt 回绝它，正好暴露出他真正追求的从来不只是钱。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常用于生意、赌博、灰色交易甚至职场跳槽，带一种“别恋战、现在退出最聪明”的语气。

sourceContext.previousLine: Marie This is a safe space. Let it all out.
sourceContext.targetLine: Let it all out.
sourceContext.nextLine: Walt, we all just want to help you.

definitions: 都说出来吧；把压着的情绪放出来
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Marie 催 Walt：别憋着了，都说出来吧。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是美国式情感干预里很典型的话，带一点治疗室语感。它假设“表达情绪”本身就是正确路径，但对像 Walt 这种把控制欲看得极重的人，反而会像被围攻。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 适合安慰哭泣、发泄、情绪爆发时的人。语气可以温柔，也可能像这里一样带点逼你表态的意味。

sourceContext.previousLine: Jesse What about the RV? I mean, how are we gonna move all that crystal product?
sourceContext.targetLine: Walter Jesse, this is your venture. You're a millionaire. You should be able to hire your own people. Go ahead, be the boss. Be a self-starter. Go get a job.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse Uh, I don't know. I don't know how to do that stuff.

definitions: 主动型的人；不用人催就能自己开动的人
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 冷嘲热讽 Jesse：你不是大老板吗？那就自己主动点，自己把事干起来啊。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: self-starter 在美国职场里是夸人的高频词，但 Walt 用在这里完全是挖苦，意思是“别再像个没人带就不会动的人”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 招聘和绩效场景常见。通常是正面词，但语气一变，也能像这里一样变成讽刺。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter This is unacceptable. I mean, it just is. I know we can do better.
sourceContext.targetLine: I know we can do better.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse Yo, Mr. White, hold up. I mean, I've been out all night.

definitions: 我们本来可以做得更好
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 盯着成品说：我们明明还可以做得更好。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把 Walt 的驱动力写得很直白。他已经有钱了，却还是无法容忍“差不多”，因为真正驱动他的是标准、控制和证明自己。
zh-CN.usageNotes: do better 很常见，可用于工作、学习、人际关系。轻则鼓励，重则批评。语气全靠上下文。

deckMetadata.title: Breaking Bad · Crazy Handful of Nothin'

summary.zh-CN: 聚焦 S01E06 场景，提取中级学习者可迁移的高价值词汇与短语。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter This is my own private domicile and I will not be harassed... bitch!
sourceContext.targetLine: This is my own private domicile and I will not be harassed... bitch!
sourceContext.nextLine: Skyler Walt?

definitions: 私人住所
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 装腔作势地吼：这是我自己的私人住所，我不接受你们骚扰。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是整季名台词之一。Walt 明明在家暴怒失控，却突然切到一种过度正式、近乎法律腔的 private domicile，显示他开始享受用语言和姿态压人。
zh-CN.usageNotes: domicile 比 home/house 正式很多，常见于法律、表格、警务语境。日常口语直接说会有明显装正式的效果。

sourceContext.previousLine: This... is my own private domicile... and I will not be harassed. Bitch! Yeah! Yeah!
sourceContext.targetLine: This is my realm!
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse Oh, hey, now. Don't bring the bathtub into this.

definitions: 这是我的地盘；这是我掌控的领域
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 喊到最后，已经不是在护家，而是在宣布：这里是我的地盘。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: realm 比 territory 更带王国感，像在给自己加冕。对学习者很有价值，因为它把 Walt 从被动受压的人，转成主动认领权力的人。
zh-CN.usageNotes: realm 常用于知识领域、幻想世界、权力范围。口语里直接说 my realm 会显得戏剧化、野心很重。

sourceContext.previousLine: How come you're... so much more awake than me?
sourceContext.targetLine: Maybe you're not. Maybe you're just in charge of your own headlights.
sourceContext.nextLine: No, we're both awake.

definitions: 掌控；负责
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 在自言自语式地试探：你只是能控制自己的那点反应，还是你真的掌控局面？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这场地下室独白非常适合学 in charge of，因为 Walt 讨论的其实就是“谁在掌控、谁只是装作掌控”。这个短语在家庭、职场、犯罪合作里都极高频。
zh-CN.usageNotes: be in charge of 可表示正式负责，也可表示现场谁说了算。比 responsible for 更多“掌控权”意味。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hank Hey, if what she says is true, you put her in hot water here, man. I mean, if she's just making this stuff up...
sourceContext.targetLine: You put her in hot water here, man.
sourceContext.nextLine: If she's just making this stuff up...

definitions: 惹上麻烦；让人处境危险
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克提醒 Walt：你这是把 Skyler 推进麻烦里去了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Skyler 跑去见 Jesse 后，Hank 立刻意识到这不是简单夫妻矛盾，而是可能让人惹祸上身。hot water 是非常地道的“陷入麻烦”表达，既可轻松也可严重。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常见于 be in hot water / get someone in hot water。适用于学校、职场、家庭、法律问题。

sourceContext.previousLine: There's your reputation. There's your end of it. There's your product. There's your market share. There's your name.
sourceContext.targetLine: And I know that once you've got your name, and once you've got your market share, then you better watch over your turf.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse Jesus, Mr. White.

definitions: 守住你的地盘
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 对街头规则的理解是：你一旦有了名号和份额，就得守住自己的地盘。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是 Heisenberg 人格正式成形的集数。turf 在毒贩、帮派、商业竞争里都很关键，不是字面草皮，而是你的势力范围和利益盘子。
zh-CN.usageNotes: turf 可用于 crime turf、sales turf、political turf。watch over your turf 带很强的守地盘意味。

sourceContext.previousLine: Maybe it can be a million dollar operation. Or two. Or three.
sourceContext.targetLine: We are a going business. We are a consignment operation.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse Yeah, but, yo, where are we gonna get that kind of cash?

definitions: 寄售式运作；先出货后结算的生意模式
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 已经不把他们当小打小闹的卖货，而是当成一门要赊货、压货、放大规模的生意。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句标志着 Walt 完全切入商业脑，而不只是制毒技术脑。consignment 是很值得学的交易词，懂了就能听懂很多关于先货后款的商业安排。
zh-CN.usageNotes: consignment 常用于零售、艺术品、二手店和灰色交易。核心是卖掉后再分钱或再结款。

sourceContext.previousLine: Jesse All right, so that's what we need. That's 140 grand. And, yo, how do you propose we raise that kind of money, man? Huh?
sourceContext.targetLine: Walter We don't. We don't need all of it, maybe just enough to pay for the ingredients and get our shipments out. Money up front for the operation.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse Well, how are we gonna get that?

definitions: 先给前期启动资金
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 盘算的是，先把开局的钱垫出来，把货转起来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是典型谈生意脑回路，不是“我要更多钱”，而是“先把启动现金打进系统”。up front 在这里是商业支付逻辑，不是地点上的前面。
zh-CN.usageNotes: up front 可指预付、坦白、直接。谈钱时 most often 就是“先付一部分/全额预付”。

summary.zh-CN: 聚焦 S01E07 场景，提取中级学习者可迁移的高价值词汇与短语。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hank By tomorrow morning, there won't be a place in this town these guys can hide.
sourceContext.targetLine: No stone will go unturned.
sourceContext.nextLine: No shit. It's just us and the feds on it?

definitions: 一处都不会放过；一定会查个底朝天
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克放话：这回绝对会查到底，一处都不放过。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是警察系统发狠时很典型的表达。它不是字面翻石头，而是强调搜查、排查、追踪会非常彻底，也让整集一开场就带上强烈追捕感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 可用于调查、求职、治疗、找房、找证据等任何“穷尽所有办法”的场景。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hank As in, how to proceed.
sourceContext.targetLine: No double-talk. No bullshit.
sourceContext.nextLine: I know you know what happened and I know that you know where he is.

definitions: 含糊其辞的鬼话；绕来绕去不说人话
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克直接压上去：别给我绕圈子，别扯废话。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 审讯 Jesse 时，Hank 用 double-talk 指那种故意讲得模糊、试图糊弄过去的说话方式。它既有警察口气，也很适合日常冲突里骂人别耍嘴皮子。
zh-CN.usageNotes: double-talk 常带指责意味，表示说话虚、绕、故意混淆。比 evasive answers 更口语。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hank Look, even if you ask for a lawyer, it just makes things that much worse.
sourceContext.targetLine: Just level with us and then we can all go home happy.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse You threatening me?

definitions: 跟我们说实话；坦白点
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克压着火气说：你就跟我们讲实话，大家都省事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是审讯里常见的软硬兼施说法，表面像给台阶，实际是在施压。level with someone 很有口语感，比 tell the truth 更像“别装了，摊牌吧”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 适用于朋友、伴侣、家人、同事，也可用于警察盘问。语气通常带“我知道你没全说”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Skinny Pete It's all about old dudes and cars. It's all old dudes and cars. It's appointment only, all very clean and legit.
sourceContext.targetLine: It's appointment only, all very clean and legit.
sourceContext.nextLine: Jesse Hmm. Car dealer.

definitions: 只接预约客；不是随便走进去就能办
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Skinny Pete 说那地方只接预约，看起来干净又正规。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 他们在找洗钱和交易的入口时，appointment only 说明这个场子靠筛客人来维持体面与安全。对中国学习者来说，这种“预约制 = 更高门槛/更私密”的社会含义很值得补上。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 美容、律师、医生、画廊、私人销售都常用。语气会带一点排他感和专业感。

sourceContext.previousLine: Skyler It's all they ever talk about. I don't know where he gets it from.
sourceContext.targetLine: Walt Jr. the kid's this geeky looking kid, but he's out for blood, you know?
sourceContext.nextLine: Skyler There he is. Mister Baby Cakes himself.

definitions: 下狠手；要狠狠干掉对方
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 小 Walter 讲拳击比赛时说：那小子看着书呆子样，但上场是真下狠手。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句表面在聊拳击，其实也呼应整集越来越凶的猎杀气氛。out for blood 在英语里非常形象，意思不是字面见血，而是带着强烈攻击欲、报复欲冲过来。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 既可用于体育、商业竞争，也可用于骂人太狠。语气很强，适合高冲突语境。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walter Oh, and the basement, you had to have known all about the basement. And that iron on the wall? Cuffs? So, you obviously wised up. Cuffs? Cuffs?
sourceContext.targetLine: So, you obviously wised up.
sourceContext.nextLine: Cuffs? Cuffs?

definitions: 终于醒悟了；终于学聪明了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Walt 试探地下室那人时说：看来你后来终于长记性了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: wise up 有一种“之前你傻，现在你总算学会了”的味道。Walt 在这里边试探边推理，用这个词很像在对一个老江湖说“你总不至于一直那么蠢吧”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 可说 Wise up! 来骂人清醒点，也可说 He finally wised up。比 realize 更带训斥和成长代价。

sourceContext.previousLine: Walt Jr. There he is. Mister Baby Cakes himself.
sourceContext.targetLine: Walter and I have come to an understanding.
sourceContext.nextLine: Skyler Have you made your decision?

definitions: 达成某种默契/共识
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Skyler 先替两人定调：我们已经谈妥了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这个表达常常故意说得很含糊，不把具体内容讲死。Skyler 在这里用它，是想把家里的决策包装成成熟共识，而不是刚吵完架的妥协。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 可用于感情、家庭、商业、外交。比 agree 更模糊，也更像“彼此心里有数”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hank Wait a minute. Huh. Hey, little pothead. What'd they say to Old Yeller? Run away? Going to live on a farm?
sourceContext.targetLine: The forbidden fruit tastes the sweetest, doesn't it? Ah, but then it... turns out to be a cheap, disgusting disappointment, huh?
sourceContext.nextLine: Never trust a drug addict.

definitions: 越不让碰越想碰的东西
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 汉克嘲讽 Jesse：禁果看着最甜，真吃到嘴里却又廉价又恶心。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这个说法带很强的圣经文化底色，指亚当夏娃的禁果，所以美国人一说 forbidden fruit，往往就暗含“明知不该还偏要去碰”的诱惑逻辑。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常用于恋爱、偷情、违禁、叛逆、消费诱惑。比 temptation 更有文化味，也更像在讲人性。
